title: USD Variant Namespace Source Contract

included: wrapping a base NamespaceSource with direct selected variant composition
computing selected variants after non-variant composition arcs have supplied their opinions
reading source-visible variantSelection fields from strength-ordered prim opinions
reading source-visible variantSetNames and variantSetChildren fields to discover authored variant sets
reading selected variant specs through base-source variant-selection paths
mapping selected variant root specs and descendants onto the variant-owning prim path
recursive or nested selected variant expansion
reuse of a previously decided selection for the same variant set when a later matching set has no authored selection
variantSelection fallback value of an empty map producing no selected variant when no previous selection exists
remapping path-valued field contents authored inside selected variant specs into the variant-owning namespace
missing or empty variant selections remaining inert
multiple direct selected variant sets ordered by the final source-visible variantSetNames order
base-source local/layer-stack and inherit opinions stronger than selected variant opinions
selected variant opinions weaker than inherits and stronger than relocates, references, payloads, and specializes by LIVERPS strength placement
effective population specifier selection across base and selected variant specs
ordered child prim name composition across base and selected variant specs
ordered property name composition across base and selected variant specs
diagnostics for missing selected variant sets and missing selected variant specs
deferred: inherits, specializes, relocates, and value clips
reference or payload evaluation inside selected variants
external variant fallback maps or stage/session policies not defined by the AOUSD v1.0.1 core composition text
cross-layer variantSelection and variantSetNames listOp composition beyond the base source
attribute value resolution and time sample interpolation

model.VariantNamespaceSource: A namespace-source provider backed by a non-variant base NamespaceSource that evaluates direct selected variants.
model.BaseNamespaceSource: The non-variant namespace input satisfying contracts/capabilities/non-variant-namespace-source-input.json, with enough strength-ordered contributing opinions to compute variant selections and insert selected variant opinions at LIVERPS strength.
model.VariantSelection: A source-visible variantSelection map entry from a prim, mapping variant set name to selected variant name.
model.VariantSetDeclaration: A source-visible variant set name from variantSetNames metadata or variantSetChildren storage.
model.VariantSpecPath: The selected variant path formed by applying a variant selection to the owning prim path, for example /World{modelingVariant=high}.
model.VariantMapping: The selected variant root path mapped to the owning prim path, with descendant prims and properties projected by replacing the selected variant prefix with the owning prim path.
model.ContributingSources: Adapter rendering of strong-to-weak ContributingOpinion records. Domain records store source identity separately from semantic spec paths.

input.baseSource: target-native non-variant NamespaceSource with strength-ordered contributing opinions

output.selectedVariants: selected variants, including recursively selected variants, with primPath, variantSet, selection, and variantPath

input.path: absolute composed prim path, variant-selection prim path, or /

output.opinions: strong-to-weak contributing opinions after selected variant opinions have been inserted at LIVERPS strength

output.exists: whether base or selected variant specs contribute a prim-like input at the path
output.childNames: composed child prim names before stage mask or active filtering
output.propertyNames: composed property names before schema fallback injection
output.contributingSources: strong-to-weak base or selected variant contributors

output.exists: whether base or selected variant specs contribute an attribute or relationship at the path
output.fields: strongest authored field per field key
output.contributingSources: strong-to-weak base or selected variant contributors

laws: the variant source consumes a non-variant base NamespaceSource satisfying the non-variant namespace-source input capability and must not require stage population to know whether opinions came from base layers, references, relocates, inherits, specializes, or selected variants
the variant source must not open files, resolve assets, or inspect parser-local USDA syntax

variant selections are computed after non-variant composition arcs have supplied their opinions, matching the AOUSD v1.0.1 requirement that variant evaluation be deferred until other composition arcs are available
variant selections are read from the strongest source-visible variantSelection map entry for the variant set in the prim's strength-ordered opinion list

if no authored selection is found for a variant set, the field fallback is the empty map and no variant is selected unless a selection for the same variant set was previously decided while composing the current selected-variant branch
a previously decided selection for a variant set must be reused when composing a later matching variant set in the same selected-variant branch
variant source records must retain owning prim paths, selected variant paths, and mapped contributor paths as semantic path handles/references rather than raw strings
variant contributingSources domain data must be typed ContributingOpinion records, not composite source:path strings

variant source records must retain variant set names and selected variant names used for identity decisions as semantic name handles; selected variant names must not be restricted to the prim/property identifier grammar
missing or empty selected variant names are inert and must not contribute variant specs
declared variant sets are discovered from the composed variantSetNames list when present, otherwise from variantSetChildren storage; variantSelection entries for undeclared sets must be diagnosed
the resulting source-visible variant set order is the selected variant set strength order; the first selected variant set in that order is stronger than later selected variant sets
the selected variant path is constructed through usd-paths by applying the selected variant set and variant name to the owning prim path
the selected variant root maps to the owning prim path, and descendants are mapped by replacing the selected variant root prefix with the owning prim path

path-valued field contents authored inside selected variant prims or properties, including listOp<ObjectPath> targetPaths and connectionPaths, must be remapped by replacing the selected variant root prefix with the owning prim path before the fields are emitted
selected-variant targetPaths and connectionPaths elements outside the selected variant subtree are pruned in this bounded provider instead of being emitted with variant-selection paths
path-valued field remapping is namespace composition and does not imply full listOp composition, dictionary merging, or attribute value resolution
base-source local and inherits opinions are stronger than selected variant opinions; specializes opinions from the base source are weaker than selected variant opinions

selected variant opinions must be inserted at LIVERPS strength: weaker than local and inherits opinions, and stronger than relocates, references, payloads, and specializes in the full composition model; a terminal usd-composition-arbitrator must merge payload opinions from the payload opinion source before claiming final payload-aware composition

the graph must feed a terminal non-variant namespace source into this source so variant selections can observe available non-variant opinions before selected variant opinions are inserted at LIVERPS strength
a missing selected variant set spec must be diagnosed with the owning prim path and selected variant set
a missing selected variant spec must be diagnosed with the owning prim path, variant set, selected variant, and expected variant path
nested variant selections authored inside selected variants are discovered from the composed selected-variant namespace and may contribute recursively selected variant specs
recursive selected variant contributors authored deeper in namespace are stronger than shallower selected variant contributors of the same arc family
references and payloads authored inside selected variants remain inert field data in this first provider
for non-special field keys in this first contract, field maps expose the strongest authored field value and do not combine dictionaries, listOps, time samples, or values
childNames returned by this source are already composition-ordered; stage population must preserve this order for sources declaring child_ordering=composed
propertyNames returned by this source are already composition-ordered; stage population must preserve this order for sources declaring property_ordering=composed

child prim names are composed with specializes contributors weaker than selected variant contributors, and local/inherits contributors stronger than selected variant contributors, applying the strongest available ordering after the strongest contribution

property names are the union of specializes, selected variant, and stronger base properties, sorted with usd-paths path element ordering, then reordered by the strongest non-specializes propertyOrder if present
adapter JSON may seed base layers for conformance tests, but domain APIs exchange NamespaceSource, path, token, and field value objects

adapter_protocol.input: A JSON file containing one case with rootLayer, optional sublayers map/list, optional referenceLayers map, and an ops array. The adapter constructs the configured terminal non-variant namespace-source chain before constructing the variant namespace source; the current bounded adapter uses layer-stack, reference, relocates, inherits, and specializes providers as that concrete input.
adapter_protocol.output: A JSON object with ok, diagnostics, and results.
